points.text: Genmab A/S maintains a pipeline of innovative antibody medicines including bispecific antibodies, antibody–drug conjugates, and immune-modulating antibodies as part of its core therapeutic strategy.

Institute of Molecular and Clinical Ophthalmology Basel (IOB) — MitoCatch-Bi — Mitochondrial delivery for neurodegenerative diseases and optic nerve degeneration — Developed bispecific binders linking mitochondria and cell surfaces to enable targeted mitochondrial transplantation in neurons, retinal, heart, endothelial, and immune cells.

Almirall filed the first Clinical Trial Application (CTA) for a FIT-IG® bispecific antibody targeting IL-13 and OX-40L for atopic dermatitis, advancing EpimAb Biotherapeutics' platform into clinical development.

EpimAb Biotherapeutics' proprietary FIT-Ig platform enables the generation of bispecific antibodies using basic monoclonal antibody structural parts without complex modifications, with multiple candidates now in clinical stages.

Partner Therapeutics received FDA acceptance of a supplemental biologics license application (sBLA) for zenocutuzumab-zbco (Bizengri), a bispecific antibody targeting HER3 and HER2, for advanced NRG1 fusion-positive cholangiocarcinoma.

The NCCN updated Clinical Practice Guidelines to include zenocutuzumab as a Category 2A subsequent-line therapy and Category 2B frontline option for NRG1 fusion-positive cholangiocarcinoma based on phase 2 eNRGy trial data.

Clinical discourse highlights BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies in multiple myeloma, with safety profiles focusing on cytokine release syndrome, infection risk, and cytopenias requiring proactive monitoring.
Relapsed large B-cell lymphoma treatment paradigms are debating CAR-T versus bispecific antibodies, weighing curative intent against real-world access barriers and off-the-shelf availability.
Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — REGN5668 — Recurrent ovarian and endometrial cancer — MUC16 × CD28 bispecific antibody evaluated in Phase 1/2 study with cemiplimab and fianlimab at SGO 2026.
Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody platform — Oncology and serious diseases — Advancing late-stage clinical pipeline of wholly owned bispecific programs alongside approved antibody medicines.

IOB — MitoCatch-Bi — Mitochondrial diseases (Parkinson's, optic nerve degeneration, heart failure) — Engineered bispecific binders successfully deliver functional mitochondria to target cells in animal models with no detectable immune reaction; mechanism described in Nature publication.
Almirall — FIT-IG® Bispecific Antibody (IL-13/OX-40L) — Atopic Dermatitis — First CTA filed by license partner; First-in-Human Phase 1 initiation expected H1 2026.
Partner Therapeutics — zenocutuzumab-zbco (Bizengri) — Advanced NRG1+ Cholangiocarcinoma — FDA received sBLA; NCCN guidelines updated to Category 2A subsequent-line and 2B frontline recommendation.

Multiple Myeloma Community — BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Safety Management — Key toxicities identified include cytokine release syndrome, infection risk, and cytopenias requiring careful monitoring in early relapse settings.

Relapsed LBCL Treatment Debate — Bispecific antibodies vs CAR-T — Relapsed large B-cell lymphoma — Strategic tradeoff defined as one-time curative CAR-T intervention versus ongoing dosing and infrastructure requirements for bispecifics.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — REGN5668 — Recurrent ovarian/endometrial cancer — Phase 1/2 dose-expansion (Module 1) assessed objective response rate per RECIST v1.1 in ovarian cohort (REGN5668 + cemiplimab + fianlimab) and endometrial cohort (REGN5668 + cemiplimab).

Genmab A/S — Bispecific antibody development — Broad antibody-based therapeutic formats — 8 approved antibody medicines; strong late-stage clinical pipeline including wholly owned bispecific programs.

Institute of Molecular and Clinical Ophthalmology Basel (IOB) — Precision mitochondrial medicine via bispecific technology — MitoCatch-Bi system — Demonstrated efficacy in rescuing cell degeneration in optic nerve atrophy models; strategy published in Nature.

EpimAb Biotherapeutics — Bispecific antibody commercialization via FIT-Ig platform — IL-13/OX-40L bispecific candidate — First CTA filed by partner Almirall; multiple bispecific molecules advanced to clinic.
Almirall — Clinical development of licensed bispecific assets — FIT-IG® IL-13/OX-40L antibody — First CTA filed under 2023 license agreement with EpimAb; Phase 1 start planned H1 2026.

Partner Therapeutics — Regulatory expansion of bispecific portfolio — zenocutuzumab (Bizengri) — sBLA accepted for cholangiocarcinoma following December 2024 accelerated approval for NSCLC and pancreatic cancer.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — Advancing MUC16 × CD28 bispecific antibody program — REGN5668 — Phase 1/2 combination trials ongoing in recurrent gynecologic malignancies with primary endpoint of objective response rate.

Genmab bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target antibody binding — Phase: Late-stage clinical development — Population: Cancer and serious disease patients — Status: Part of 8 approved medicines and advancing wholly owned pipeline.

MitoCatch-Bi — Mechanism: Bispecific binders linking mitochondria to cell surfaces for targeted delivery — Phase: Preclinical (animal models) — Population: Neurons, retinal ganglion cells, heart, endothelial, and immune cells — Comparator: Traditional mitochondrial transplantation methods — Results: Improved survival of neurons from optic nerve atrophy patients; functional mitochondria observed moving, fusing, and dividing post-delivery.

FIT-IG® IL-13/OX-40L Bispecific — Mechanism: Dual targeting of IL-13 and OX-40L via Fabs-In-Tandem Immunoglobulin platform — Phase: 1 (First-in-Human) — Population: Atopic dermatitis patients — Comparator: Not specified — Endpoints: Safety and tolerability; initiation expected H1 2026.

Zenocutuzumab — Mechanism: HER3/HER2 bispecific antibody — Phase: 2 (eNRGy trial) — Population: Advanced NRG1 fusion-positive cholangiocarcinoma — Results: 36.8% overall response rate (95% CI, 16.3%-61.6%); median duration of response 12.9 months.

BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: T-cell engager targeting BCMA — Phase: Clinical use in multiple myeloma — Population: Early relapse multiple myeloma — Safety Profile: Manageable cytokine release syndrome, infection risk, and cytopenias requiring proactive monitoring.

Bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: Off-the-shelf T-cell engager — Phase: Evolving combination strategies — Population: Relapsed large B-cell lymphoma — Comparison: Versus CAR-T regarding durability and access barriers.

REGN5668 — Mechanism: MUC16 × CD28 bispecific antibody engaging T-cells via CD28 and targeting MUC16-expressing tumors — Phase: 1/2 — Population: Recurrent ovarian and endometrial cancer — Comparator: None specified in abstract — Endpoints: Objective response rate (RECIST v1.1) and safety outcomes.

EpimAb Biotherapeutics / Almirall — License agreement expansion — FIT-IG® bispecific platform — Almirall secured rights to develop up to three undisclosed bispecific target pairs; first candidate (IL-13/OX-40L) advanced to CTA filing.
